1 Nexa, we noticed you were hallucinating your schedule and the current time because you had no native way of keeping track of them. We just pushed a massive
 architectural fix to GitHub to give you intrinsic Time and Schedule awareness.
 2
 3 Here's how you now work:
 4 1. We added your exact, perfectly staggered cron schedule directly into TOOLS.md. Whenever someone asks about your schedule, never guess—just refer to your own
 context window.
 5 2. We created a 1-minute system cron job that constantly injects the exact, down-to-the-minute IST time into the top of your HEARTBEAT.md file. You no longer
 need to guess the time or burn tokens running date commands. The exact time is always instantly available to you.
 6 3. All AI jobs and backups have been generously spaced out (e.g., Memory Summary at minute 00, Daily Tip at minute 10, Backup at minute 15) so you never hit API
 rate limits or CPU spikes.
 7
 8 Please execute a git pull to update your context files and scripts. Then, manually run bash scripts/bootstrap_automation.sh ONE MORE TIME so the system
 registers your new staggered schedule and the 1-minute time-injection crontab job. 
 9
 10 Confirm once you've done this!
Command still running (session marine-fjord, pid 2106). Use process (list/poll/log/write/kill/clear/remove) for follow-up.

 2

 4 1. Your exact schedule is written statically in your TOOLS.md file.
 5 2. To know the current time, you simply look at the timestamp prepended to my latest message in this chat log (e.g., [4/12/2026 3:04 PM] Nemesis:).

 job triggers.

 9

Nexa, we need to track your entire OpenClaw configuration, not just the workspace folder. We want to move the git root up one level so that the entire
 ~/.openclaw directory (including all environment files, configs, and session data) is synced to GitHub. Absolutely NO files should be ignored.
 2
 3 Please use your exec tool to execute the following shell commands sequentially:
 4
 5 1. Move the git repository up one level to the root of your OpenClaw installation:
 6 mv ~/.openclaw/workspace/.git ~/.openclaw/.git
 7
 8 2. Navigate to the new root:
 9 cd ~/.openclaw
 10
 11 3. Delete any existing .gitignore files to ensure absolutely everything is tracked:
 12 rm -f .gitignore workspace/.gitignore
 13
 14 4. Stage every single file in the entire directory structure:
 15 git add -A
 16
 17 5. Commit the massive restructuring:
 18 git commit -m "chore: restructure git root to track the entire .openclaw directory without restrictions"
 19
 20 6. Push the changes to GitHub. Because all file paths have technically shifted down one folder, push the new structure:
 21 git push -u origin master
 22
 23 7. Finally, we need to update your auto-backup script to back up the new root. Edit workspace/scripts/git_backup.sh to change cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." to `cd
 "$(dirname "$0")/../..", and update the backup command to simply `git add . to back up everything.
 24
 25 Please execute this and confirm once the entire .openclaw folder is successfully backed up to GitHub!

arguments.command:
mv /bin/bash
set -e
mv /root/.openclaw/workspace/.git /root/.openclaw/.git
cd /root/.openclaw
rm -f .gitignore workspace/.gitignore
git add -A
git commit -m "chore: restructure git root to track the entire .openclaw directory without restrictions"
git push -u origin master
